reasoning: Fabiano Caruana's Elo rating was recently 2789.0. For his rating to be 1% higher, it needs to increase by at least 27.89 points, reaching roughly 2817. Gaining 28 rating points at the super-GM level requires scoring roughly +5 to +6 points above expected results against extreme competition. Since Wikipedia's 'FIDE rankings' generally updates once a month when FIDE releases official lists (the 1st of every month), the rating on Nov 16 will be practically identical to Nov 9. The December and February horizons involve 1 and 3 months of potential classical chess events, respectively. Achieving a ~2817 Elo is theoretically possible if he posts historically phenomenal performances (e.g., similar to his 2014 Sinquefield Cup run), but base rates for such +28 point jumps in a short timeframe for a mature 2790-rated player are extremely small. Probabilities are scaled to reflect the near-impossibility of a mid-month Wikipedia update and low likelihoods of massive Elo gain over 1 to 3 months.
batch_queries: According to Wikipedia, will Fabiano Caruana have an Elo rating on each resolution date listed below that's at least 1% higher than on 2025-11-09?
